item.title: PG&E Cuts Power to Some Customers in California on Fire Risk
item.description: PG&E Corp. cut power to some customers in California due to dry and windy conditions that have elevated the risk of wildfires.

item.title: China Burns More Fossil Fuels for Power as Coal Production Slips
item.description: China’s thermal power generation grew for a fourth straight month in April even as coal output fell, highlighting the challenges Beijing faces in continuing to buffer its economy from the global energy crisis stemming from the war in Iran.

item.title: China Polysilicon Maker Turns to Battery Material in Solar Slump
item.description: GCL Technology Holdings Ltd., a major Chinese producer of polysilicon, is pivoting into battery materials as the solar sector remains mired in overcapacity and losses.

item.title: Disaster Resilience Investment Has Touched $100 Billion in Asia
item.description: Governments, investors and banks have poured about $100 billion into efforts to handle the effects of climate change in Asia in the past five years, with water and infrastructure projects attracting the majority of the capital.

item.title: Danish Fund, GPSC Said to Mull Sale of Taiwan Offshore Wind Farm
item.description: Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners and Thailand’s Global Power Synergy Pcl are considering selling their offshore wind farm in Taiwan, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iran War Boosts Interest in Air-to-Water Technology
item.description: Startup Atoco now has a commercial prototype.

item.title: Grid Connection Delays Threaten UK’s Offshore Wind Push
item.description: The majority of the UK’s recently announced offshore wind farms are stuck in a queue waiting for grid connections, despite a government push to speed up investment.

item.title: South Africa Expands Coal Mine to Feed Power Station for Years
item.description: South Africa’s Exxaro Resources Ltd. opened the expansion of a coal mine at its Matla operations that will keep a nearby power station supplied for nearly two more decades.

item.title: Carney Unveils Major Plan to Double Canada’s Electricity Production
item.description: The plan will loosen rules clean power rules.

item.title: China’s MMG Bemoans Congo Cobalt Curbs That Threaten Investment
item.description: China’s MMG Ltd. wants more clarity on the Democratic Republic of Congo’s cobalt-export policy after the state-controlled miner received a disappointing quota, as questions mount over how curbs have been imposed.

item.title: Pollution From Coal Is Hurting Global Solar Output, Study Finds
item.description: Pollution from coal-fired power plants that can block sunlight is significantly cutting global solar output, and potentially causing an overestimation of climate progress, according to a new study.

item.title: India Sees Monsoon Rains Starting Early, Aiding Crop Sowing
item.description: India expects its monsoon rains to arrive early this year, offering some relief for farmers who rely on the seasonal showers for timely sowing of crops like rice and soybeans.

item.title: PE Firm Lightrock Raises $500 Million for New Clean Energy Fund
item.description: Private equity firm Lightrock, which was spun out from Liechtenstein’s LGT Group, has raised a new $500 million fund to back businesses advancing access to affordable and clean energy in Asia and Africa.

item.title: Clean Energy Seen as ‘Structurally Immune’ to Hormuz-Style Shock
item.description: The war in Iran has provided new impetus to the low-carbon transition as renewable energy is seen as less vulnerable to future price shocks, according to a group of corporate bosses and senior bankers.

item.title: Carney Leaves Fate of 2030 Emission Reduction Target Unclear
item.description: Canada’s target to sharply reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 is in question after Prime Minister Mark Carney said his government will “update” its climate plan, without offering further detail.

item.title: World’s Biggest Climate Fund Says UK Halves Planned Contribution
item.description: The world’s largest state-backed climate fund will lose half its funding from the UK, a major setback following the cancellation of a $4 billion pledge from the US last year.

item.title: Trump EPA Proposes Rescinding Wastewater Limits for Coal Plants
item.description: The Trump administration is formally proposing to roll back wastewater limits for coal-fired power plants.

item.title: US Southern Communities Left Reeling From Energy Grant Cancellations
item.description: The Trump administration called it DEI

item.title: El Niño Odds Rise as Storm and Crop Threats Intensify Globally
item.description: The odds are rising that a weather-roiling El Niño will emerge in the next few months and strengthen through the year, threatening global crop supplies, altering storm patterns and pushing temperatures toward record highs.

item.title: Nuveen Backs Startup With $500 Million of Funds for Clean Energy
item.description: A clean-energy project finance startup received a $500 million debt facility from Nuveen Energy Infrastructure Credit to fund environmentally friendly projects.

item.title: Early Nvidia Investor Backs Startup to Tap Idle Grid Power
item.description: A startup working to unlock excess power grid capacity for data centers raised $64 million as utilities and hyperscalers race to ease an energy crunch constraining the build-out of artificial intelligence.

item.title: UN Climate Chief Lavishes Praise on China as Trump Meets Xi
item.description: China is reaping benefits as a global leader on the green transition and other nations should follow as the Iran war shows the vulnerability of a fossil fuel-based economy, according to the top United Nations climate official.

item.title: The Next Era of Geopolitical Stability Will Be Powered by Electricity
item.description: The world's response to the Iran war energy shock will shape our climate future. Eurasia Group Chairman Gerald Butts discusses how to pick the best path forward.

item.title: India’s Top Banks Failing to Tackle Climate Risk as Threats Grow
item.description: India’s largest banks need to do more to incorporate climate risks into lending decisions as the country suffers escalating impacts from the effects of global warming, according to a new analysis.

item.title: Swiss Say Nuclear Plants Are Economically Viable for 80 Years
item.description: Switzerland said its nuclear power plants in Goesgen and Leibstadt can profitably run for as long as 80 years, two decades beyond previous projections.

item.title: Stegra and Its Lenders Agree to Unlock €1.5 Billion Debt Funding
item.description: Stegra AB has agreed with creditors to unlock about €1.5 billion ($1.8 billion) of undrawn credit lines which, together with recently secured equity funding, will help complete what will be the world’s biggest green-steel plant.

item.title: What Jinko Solar’s US Exit Means for Clean-Tech US Investment
item.description: The US has become a more hostile environment for Chinese clean-tech manufacturers.

item.title: Why More Intense Bursts of Rain Are Making the Planet Drier
item.description: Land can’t absorb all the water from strong, sporadic storms, leaving much of it to evaporate. That’s happening at a global scale, according to new research.

item.title: Nuclear Power’s Second Revolution Needs More Fuel
item.description: New reactors with new designs mean new uranium mines and new production. Bloomberg Primer explores whether the industry can keep up with demand.

item.title: China’s $3 Billion US Clean Tech Exit Is an Investment Warning
item.description: Some of the world’s top green manufacturers are abandoning ambitions in the US as policies target foreign entities.
